thought_beat_text: The countdown is ticking and Snake knows the weight of the device in his hands is real weight, real consequence—and also knows, somewhere behind that knowing, that the street he's standing on has edges he can almost see if he looks sideways at it. Both things are true at the same time and he has stopped trying to resolve them, which is its own kind of discipline, or its own kind of surrender.
residue_summary: Snake is holding the mission and the seam simultaneously, and the countdown is not waiting for him to decide which one is more real.
image_hint: figure mid-stride on a wet street, timer reflected in a puddle, horizon slightly too sharp
audio_hint: low urgent pulse beneath ambient city noise, a frequency that doesn't quite belong to either
